brand_dna.category: Patented color-blind correction eyewear - sunglasses and glasses that expand color vision for people with color vision deficiency.
brand_dna.core_belief: Color Is Here - bringing color vision eyewear solutions to all and making a meaningful contribution to the human experience through boundary-breaking color vision science.
proof_points: Independent, Berkeley, California-based company with patented lens technology
Invented by a Ph.D. glass scientist and UC Berkeley-trained mathematician
Received an SBIR grant from the NIH and the 2016 Tibbets Award from the U.S. Small Business Administration
Backed by 8 peer-reviewed studies; trusted by over 2,000,000 people with CVD
brand_dna.positioning: Premium, science-first specialty eyewear - a category creator competing on proven lens technology and craftsmanship, not price.

voice_pillars.name: Science-Backed
voice_pillars.desc: Leads with neuroscience, research, and credible proof rather than hype.
voice_pillars.quote: Based on the latest color perception neuroscience, EnChroma lenses filter light

voice_pillars.name: Emotionally Honest
voice_pillars.desc: Sets real expectations and honors that each person's experience is different.
voice_pillars.quote: Everyone's perceptual processing is different. There is no one reaction

voice_pillars.name: Quietly Premium
voice_pillars.desc: Speaks to craftsmanship and understated design without shouting.
voice_pillars.quote: Wrap sport, quietly done. Square-front, wide-open vision.

voice_pillars.name: Wonder-Driven
voice_pillars.desc: Frames the product as a doorway to seeing the world in a new way.
voice_pillars.quote: See The World In A Way You Never Imagined Possible

phrases: changed, literally, the way I view the world
color like you've never seen before
See more symbols in this set?
deeper, richer and more vibrant colors
Give a new world of color a shot

icp: Adults (and parents of kids 5+) with red-green color vision deficiency who value science-proven solutions and are willing to invest in premium eyewear for the chance to experience a fuller range of color.

personas.name: First-Sight Seeker
personas.description: Someone with lifelong color blindness chasing the emotional 'EnChroma moment' - moved by the promise of seeing color and reassured by the 60-day risk-free guarantee.

personas.name: Outdoor Enthusiast
personas.description: Active, sun-and-surf lover who wants performance sport frames and sharper color outdoors - moved by durability, UV protection, and the O'Neill collaboration.

personas.name: The Gift Giver
personas.description: A partner or parent buying for a loved one with CVD - moved by the gift guide, guarantee, and the transformative story behind the lenses.

products_ladder.title: Ellis Smooth Black Outdoor Deutan Plano

offers: Pay in 4 interest-free installments with Klarna

allowed.claim: Approximately 80% of people with red-green color blindness will see an expanded range of visible colors.
Patented lens technology can improve color vision for those with red-green color blindness (UIW study).
Backed by 8 peer-reviewed studies; trusted by over 2,000,000 people with CVD.
Engineered in California, Made in Italy.
Received an SBIR grant from the NIH and the 2016 Tibbets Award.

banned.claim: Cures or fixes color blindness / restores normal color vision.
banned.why: Medical overreach - the brand states results vary and only ~80% see an expanded range, not a cure.

banned.claim: Helps you pass the Ishihara or official color blind tests.
banned.why: Not supported - their own FAQ addresses this and cannot promise it; making it a claim would be misleading.
FTC Green Guides - unqualified sustainability claim with no verifiable backing on site.
FTC Green Guides - specific recycled/carbon claim with no evidence provided in the input.

banned.claim: Relieves eye strain, headaches, or corrects vision conditions.
banned.why: Medical/therapeutic claim not stated or substantiated on the site.

banned.claim: Works for everyone who is color blind.
banned.why: Contradicts the brand's own stated ~80% figure and 'results may vary' language.
